title: Auth Core - Service, Model and Environment Setup
tech: Node.js, TypeScript, Express, PostgreSQL, Prisma, bcrypt, JWT, Zod, Vitest
keyConstraints: TypeScript strict, server-side validation with Zod, no trust of frontend input, Prisma for all DB queries
description: Implement the core authentication foundation: Prisma User model with roles, AuthService with real database queries, bcrypt password hashing, JWT token generation and verification utilities, and startup environment variable validation. No routes or middleware in this task — just the service layer and data model.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma User model has id, email, passwordHash, role (Admin/Dispatcher/Driver), createdAt, updatedAt
Prisma RefreshToken model has id, token (hashed), userId, expiresAt, revokedAt, createdAt
AuthService.login(email, password) queries real DB, verifies bcrypt hash, returns user or throws
AuthService.createTokenPair(userId, role) returns signed JWT access token (15min) and refresh token (7 days)
AuthService.verifyAccessToken(token) verifies and returns decoded payload or throws
AuthService.rotateRefreshToken(token) validates token against DB, revokes old, issues new, detects reuse
AuthService.revokeRefreshToken(token) marks token as revoked in DB
Startup validation checks JWT_SECRET, JWT_REFRESH_SECRET, DATABASE_URL are set — crashes with clear message if missing
Passwords never stored or logged in plain text
All DB operations use Prisma — no raw SQL
securityConsiderations: Refresh tokens must be stored hashed in DB (SHA-256), not plain. Token reuse detection: if a revoked refresh token is presented, revoke the entire token family. bcrypt cost factor minimum 12.
